skill.name: plans-handler
skill.description: Guide code updates for subscription plan schemas, pricing tables, quotas, and current-plan access in a product codebase.
skill.summary: Coordinate subscription plan schema, quota, pricing, and access updates.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and a weak cryptographic algorithm, but review found markdown inline code references only. No shell execution, cryptographic code, network calls, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md or reference.md.

content.value_statement: Subscription plan changes often touch database schemas, validation, forms, pricing pages, and access checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise map for coordinated plan updates.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the main files involved in adding a new quota.
Explains how database, validation, and form schemas should stay aligned.
Points pricing table work toward plan API data and subscribe URL helpers.
Documents client and server patterns for accessing the current user plan.
Encourages dashboard-based plan updates instead of manual seed changes.
limitations: It is tailored to one codebase structure and file layout.
It does not implement payment provider integrations by itself.
It does not verify live plan data or provider identifiers.
It does not include automated tests or executable scripts.

use_cases.title: Add a Product Quota
use_cases.description: Update the quota schema, validation schema, and admin form for a new usage limit.

use_cases.title: Build a Pricing Table
use_cases.description: Create pricing UI that reads plan data, shows quotas, and links users to subscription checkout.

use_cases.title: Review Plan Access Logic
use_cases.description: Check whether client and server code use the documented current-plan helpers.

prompt_templates.title: Find Plan Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plans-handler skill to identify the files I should inspect before changing subscription plan quotas.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Quota
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plans-handler skill to add a new usage quota. Keep the database schema, validation schema, and form fields consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Create Pricing UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plans-handler skill to build a pricing table that fetches plans, displays quotas, and uses the subscribe URL helper.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Plan Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plans-handler skill to review this branch for plan schema drift, missing quota fields, and incorrect current-plan access.

output_examples.input: Add a monthly image quota to plans.
output_examples.output: The assistant would recommend a usage-based quota name, update the plan schema, update validation, add the form input, and ask the user to update plan data through the admin dashboard.

output_examples.input: Build a pricing page for existing plans.
output: Fetch plans from the application API.
Render feature and quota values from plan data.
Use the subscribe URL helper for checkout buttons.

output_examples.input: Check whether server code can read the user's plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant would look for the authenticated server wrapper and current-plan helper, then flag direct or inconsistent plan lookups.

best_practices: Keep database schema, validation schema, and admin form fields synchronized.
Use usage-based quota names instead of time-bound names.
Treat the super-admin dashboard as the source of truth for plan data.
anti_patterns: Do not manually seed plan data when the dashboard should manage it.
Do not add form fields without matching schema and validation changes.
Do not hardcode checkout URLs when a subscribe URL helper exists.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps coordinate code changes for subscription plans, quotas, pricing tables, and current-plan access.

faq.question: Can it add a payment provider?
faq.answer: No. It references provider identifiers, but it does not implement Stripe, Dodo, or PayPal integrations.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain instructions and markdown references only.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it specific to one repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The file paths and helper names are specific to the source codebase.

faq.question: How should plan data be updated?
faq.answer: The skill recommends using the super-admin plans dashboard as the source of truth.
